brand.name: VeryKit

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Browse all available tools, organized by category

about.title: About VeryKit
about.description: VeryKit was born from the need to have a single, clean, fast, and privacy-conscious place for the small utilities that QA Engineers, Test Automation Engineers, and Software Developers use every day. No ads, no tracking, no paywalls. Just tools that work, run locally, and disappear when you're done.

about.title: About VeryKit
about.description: VeryKit was born from the need to have a single, clean, fast, and privacy-conscious place for the small utilities that QA Engineers, Test Automation Engineers, and Software Developers use every day.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: VeryKit exists to eliminate the friction of everyday development tasks. Every tool runs entirely in your browser, meaning your data never leaves your device. We believe that utility tools should be fast, accessible, and privacy-respecting by default.

privacy.title: Privacy First
privacy.description: No analytics, no tracking, no server-side processing. Every computation happens locally using modern browser APIs like the Web Crypto API. When you close the tab, everything disappears.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: VeryKit is built in the open. Contributions, bug reports, and feature requests are welcome from the community. Check our contributing guidelines to get started.

contributing.title: Contributing
contributing.description: Thank you for your interest in contributing to VeryKit! Here's how you can help.

reporting.title: Reporting Issues
reporting.description: Found a bug or have a suggestion? Open an issue on our GitHub repository. Please include:
items: A clear description of the problem or suggestion
Steps to reproduce (for bugs)
Your browser and operating system
Screenshots if applicable

code.title: Contributing Code
code.description: Want to add a new tool or fix a bug? Here's the process:
items: Fork the repository on GitHub
Create a feature branch from main
Follow the existing project patterns (see src/features/ for examples)
Add tests for your changes
Ensure all tests pass with npm test
Submit a pull request with a clear description

setup.title: Development Setup
setup.description: Clone the repository, install dependencies, and start the development server:
setup.command: git clone https://github.com/TioBrock/VeryKit.git && cd VeryKit && npm install && npm run dev

security.title: Security
security.description: Your security is important to us. This page explains how to report vulnerabilities and what we do to keep VeryKit safe.

reporting.title: Reporting Vulnerabilities
reporting.description: If you discover a security vulnerability in VeryKit, please report it responsibly:
items: Open a private security advisory on our GitHub repository
Do not disclose the vulnerability publicly until a fix is available
Include a detailed description of the issue and potential impact
We will acknowledge your report within 48 hours

design.title: Security Design
design.description: VeryKit is designed with security in mind:
items: Client-side only: All computations happen in your browser. No data is ever sent to a server.
No data collection: We do not collect, store, or transmit any user data.
No tracking: No analytics, cookies, or tracking scripts.
Web Crypto API: Cryptographic operations use the browser's built-in secure APIs.
Open source: Our code is publicly auditable on GitHub.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: The following are considered in scope for security reports:
items: Vulnerabilities in the web application code
Issues with data handling or processing logic
Cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities
Any issue that could compromise user privacy

footer.description: Free, open source, privacy-conscious tools for everyday QA and development work.

uuid-generator.title: UUID Generator
uuid-generator.description: Generate random UUIDs (v4) instantly in your browser. No data leaves your device.
uuid-generator.resultPlaceholder: Click "Generate UUID" to create a new identifier
uuid-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate a UUID for a new database record
Create unique identifiers for API request tracking
Generate test data for QA and development

items.question: What is a UUID?
items.answer: A UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) is a 128-bit identifier that is unique across space and time. The v4 format generates a completely random identifier.

items.question: Does this tool send data to a server?
items.answer: No. All UUID generation happens locally in your browser using the Web Crypto API. No data is sent anywhere.

items.question: What is the difference between UUID v4 and other versions?
items.answer: UUID v4 is completely random. Other versions like v1 use timestamps and MAC addresses, while v5 uses namespace-based hashing. V4 is the most commonly used format for general purposes.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When you need a unique identifier for database records
When generating test data for API development or QA testing
When creating unique IDs for logging, tracking, or session management
When working on distributed systems that require collision-resistant identifiers

password-generator.title: Password Generator
password-generator.description: Generate secure random passwords instantly in your browser. Fully customizable character options. No data leaves your device.
password-generator.resultPlaceholder: Click "Generate Password" to create a new password
password-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.
password-generator.excludeAmbiguous: Exclude ambiguous characters (1, l, I, O, 0)
password-generator.errorNoChars: Please select at least one character type.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Create a strong password for a new online account
Generate test passwords for QA and security testing
Create API keys or tokens for development environments

items.question: How secure are the generated passwords?
items.answer: The passwords are generated using the Web Crypto API, which provides cryptographically secure random values. They are generated entirely in your browser and never sent to any server.

items.question: Can I customize the password length?
items.answer: Yes. You can set the password length from 4 to 128 characters using the length input field.

items.question: What character types can I include?
items.answer: You can toggle uppercase letters (A-Z), lowercase letters (a-z), numbers (0-9), and symbols (!@#$%^&* etc.). At least one type must be selected.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When you need a strong, random password for a new account
When generating test credentials for QA and security testing
When creating API tokens, secret keys, or session identifiers
When you want to verify password strength requirements

lorem-ipsum-generator.title: Lorem Ipsum Generator
lorem-ipsum-generator.description: Generate placeholder text in paragraphs, words, or lists for design mockups and development. No data leaves your device.
lorem-ipsum-generator.resultPlaceholder: Click "Generate Text" to create placeholder content
lorem-ipsum-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Fill a web page layout with placeholder text during design
Generate sample content for blog or CMS testing
Create text blocks for UI component testing and QA

items.question: What is Lorem Ipsum?
items.answer: Lorem Ipsum is simply dummy text of the printing and typesetting industry. It has been the industry's standard dummy text since the 1500s.

items.question: How many items can I generate at once?
items.answer: You can generate from 1 to 100 items (paragraphs, words, or list items) in a single operation.

items.question: Is the generated text random?
items.answer: The paragraphs and lists are drawn from the classic Lorem Ipsum corpus. Words are randomly selected from the corpus to ensure variety.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When designing page layouts and needing placeholder text
When testing text-heavy components like articles or feeds
When creating mockups for CMS or blogging platforms
When developing responsive components that need variable content length

cpf-generator.title: CPF Generator & Validator
cpf-generator.description: Generate and validate Brazilian CPF numbers with the correct check digits. Fully client-side, no data sent anywhere.
cpf-generator.resultPlaceholder: Click "Generate CPF" or enter a CPF to validate
cpf-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate valid CPF numbers for QA testing of Brazilian systems
Validate user-entered CPFs in form validation logic
Create test data for financial or government applications

items.question: What is a CPF?
items.answer: CPF (Cadastro de Pessoas Físicas) is the Brazilian individual taxpayer registration number. It consists of 11 digits, including two check digits calculated using a specific algorithm.

items.question: Are the generated CPFs real?
items.answer: No. The generator creates CPF numbers that pass the check digit algorithm but are not necessarily registered with the Brazilian IRS (Receita Federal). They are for testing purposes only.

items.question: How does validation work?
items.answer: Validation checks that the CPF has exactly 11 digits and that the two check digits match the values calculated from the first 9 digits using the official algorithm.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When testing Brazilian forms that require CPF input
When validating CPF entries in QA and automation testing
When generating test data for financial or government systems
When developing CPF validation logic and need reference values

json-formatter.title: JSON Formatter & Minifier
json-formatter.description: Format, validate, and minify JSON data instantly. Configurable indentation. No data leaves your device.
json-formatter.resultPlaceholder: Formatted or minified JSON will appear here
json-formatter.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Format an API response for better readability
Minify a JSON payload before sending it over the network
Validate JSON data and locate syntax errors

items.question: What is JSON formatting?
items.answer: JSON formatting (or pretty-printing) adds indentation and line breaks to JSON data, making it human-readable. Minifying removes all unnecessary whitespace.

items.question: What indentation sizes are supported?
items.answer: You can choose between 2 or 4 spaces for indentation, or use tab characters.

items.question: Does this tool work with large JSON files?
items.answer: Yes, the formatting happens entirely in your browser. However, very large files (over 10MB) may take a moment to process depending on your device.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When debugging API responses and need readable JSON
When preparing JSON data for configuration files
When validating JSON before sending it to an API
When reducing payload size by minifying JSON data

base64-converter.title: Base64 Encode / Decode
base64-converter.description: Encode and decode Base64 strings instantly in your browser. Supports UTF-8 text. No data leaves your device.
base64-converter.resultPlaceholder: Encoded or decoded text will appear here
base64-converter.inputPlaceholder: Enter text to encode or decode...
base64-converter.errorDecode: Failed to decode. The input is not valid Base64.
base64-converter.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Encode credentials for HTTP Basic Authentication headers
Decode Base64-encoded API responses or tokens
Convert binary data to a text-safe format for transmission

items.question: What is Base64?
items.answer: Base64 is an encoding scheme that represents binary data as a string of ASCII characters. It is commonly used to encode data for transmission over text-based protocols like HTTP and email.

items.question: Does this tool support UTF-8 text?
items.answer: Yes. The encoder and decoder fully support UTF-8, including accented characters, emojis, and non-Latin scripts.

items.question: Is Base64 encoding secure?
items.answer: No. Base64 is an encoding, not encryption. It is easily reversible and should not be used to protect sensitive data. Use it for data transport, not security.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When encoding credentials for HTTP Basic Authentication
When decoding Base64-encoded tokens, payloads, or emails
When embedding binary data (images, files) in JSON or XML
When debugging data encoding issues in APIs

url-encoder.title: URL Encode / Decode
url-encoder.description: Encode and decode URL parameters and special characters instantly. Supports full and component encoding. No data leaves your device.
url-encoder.resultPlaceholder: Encoded or decoded URL will appear here
url-encoder.inputPlaceholder: Enter a URL or text to encode/decode...
url-encoder.errorDecode: Failed to decode. The input is not a valid encoded URL.
url-encoder.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Encode query parameters before constructing a URL
Decode a URL-encoded string from an API response
Sanitize user input for use in URL paths

items.question: What is URL encoding?
items.answer: URL encoding (percent-encoding) replaces unsafe characters with a % followed by two hexadecimal digits. For example, a space becomes %20.

items.question: What is the difference between Full and Component encoding?
items.answer: Full encoding (encodeURI) preserves path separators like / and :. Component encoding (encodeURIComponent) encodes everything except letters, digits, and _ . - ~.

items.question: When should I use URL encoding?
items.answer: Use it when passing data in URL query strings, especially for values containing spaces, special characters, or non-ASCII text.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When building URLs with dynamic query parameters
When decoding URL-encoded strings from API responses or logs
When sanitizing user input that will be used in URLs
When debugging URL encoding/decoding issues

case-converter.title: Case Converter
case-converter.description: Convert text between UPPERCASE, lowercase, camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case, PascalCase and more. No data leaves your device.
case-converter.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.
case-converter.errorEmpty: Please enter some text to convert.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Convert variable names between camelCase and snake_case when porting code
Format text for headers, titles, or documentation
Normalize inconsistent naming conventions in a codebase

items.question: What is case conversion?
items.answer: Case conversion transforms text between different naming conventions used in programming and writing, such as camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case, and others.

items.question: Which case should I use?
items.answer: It depends on context: camelCase for JavaScript variables, snake_case for Python/Ruby, kebab-case for CSS and URLs, PascalCase for class names, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants.

items.question: Can I convert entire code snippets?
items.answer: Yes, the converter works with any text input. However, it converts all words uniformly, so it's best suited for single identifiers or phrases.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When renaming variables between different language conventions
When normalizing text for documentation or content management
When converting CSS class names or URL slugs
When generating constant names or enum values from descriptive text

timestamp-converter.title: Timestamp / Unix Time Converter
timestamp-converter.description: Convert between Unix timestamps and human-readable dates. Supports seconds and milliseconds, UTC and local time. No data leaves your device.
timestamp-converter.inputPlaceholder: Enter a Unix timestamp or date string...
timestamp-converter.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Convert a Unix timestamp from an API response to a readable date
Get the current Unix timestamp for logging or debugging
Validate timestamp values in QA and automated testing

items.question: What is a Unix timestamp?
items.answer: A Unix timestamp is the number of seconds (or milliseconds) that have elapsed since January 1, 1970 (the Unix epoch). It is a widely used standard for representing dates and times in computing.

items.question: What is the difference between seconds and milliseconds?
items.answer: A timestamp in seconds has 10 digits (e.g., 1700000000). A timestamp in milliseconds has 13 digits (e.g., 1700000000000). The tool auto-detects the format.

items.question: What date formats are accepted?
items.answer: The tool accepts ISO 8601 format (2024-01-15T10:30:00Z) and common date formats. You can also paste a Unix timestamp directly.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When debugging API responses that contain Unix timestamps
When converting log timestamps to human-readable dates
When generating current timestamps for testing or documentation
When validating time-related data in QA and automation

jwt-decoder.title: JWT Decoder
jwt-decoder.description: Decode JSON Web Tokens (JWT) instantly. View Header, Payload, and Signature. Detects expired tokens. All processing happens client-side.
jwt-decoder.errorInvalid: Invalid JWT token. Please check the format.
jwt-decoder.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Debug and inspect JWT tokens from API responses
Verify token claims and expiration dates
Understand the structure of authentication tokens

items.question: What is a JWT?
items.answer: A JWT (JSON Web Token) is a compact, URL-safe means of representing claims between two parties. It consists of three parts: header, payload, and signature, separated by dots.

items.question: Does this tool validate JWT signatures?
items.answer: No. This tool only decodes the token contents for inspection. It does not verify the cryptographic signature, as that requires the signing key.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When debugging authentication issues in API calls
When you need to inspect token contents quickly
When checking if a JWT token has expired
When learning about JWT structure and claims

json-compare.title: JSON Compare
json-compare.description: Compare two JSON objects side by side. Highlights added, removed, and changed values visually.
json-compare.errorInvalid: One or both inputs are not valid JSON.
json-compare.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.
json-compare.errorEmpty: Please enter JSON in both fields.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Compare two API responses to find differences
Validate configuration file changes
Review JSON payload modifications before deployment

items.question: How does JSON comparison work?
items.answer: The tool recursively traverses both JSON objects and compares each key-value pair. Differences are categorized as added (new keys), removed (missing keys), or changed (different values).

items.question: Does order of array elements matter?
items.answer: Yes. Array comparison is order-sensitive. Two arrays with the same elements in different order will be flagged as different.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When debugging API response differences
When comparing configuration files
When reviewing JSON data changes

credit-card-generator.title: Credit Card Test Generator
credit-card-generator.description: Generate valid test credit card numbers for Visa, Mastercard, Amex, and Discover. For sandbox/testing only - not real cards.
credit-card-generator.resultPlaceholder: Click Generate to create a test card number
credit-card-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.
credit-card-generator.warning: TESTING ONLY: These are dummy card numbers for sandbox environments. Never use real card numbers.

Create dummy data for QA testing of checkout flows

items.question: Are these real credit card numbers?
items.answer: No. These are valid test card numbers that pass the Luhn algorithm check but are not associated with any real accounts. They are intended for testing payment forms in sandbox environments.

items.question: Which card brands are supported?
items.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express (Amex), and Discover.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When testing payment form validation
When integrating with payment gateways in sandbox mode
When creating test data for QA automation of checkout flows

credit-card-generator.cardCvvNote: For testing only - not verified by payment gateways

cnpj-generator.title: CNPJ Generator & Validator
cnpj-generator.description: Generate and validate Brazilian CNPJ numbers with correct check digits. Fully client-side, no data sent anywhere.
cnpj-generator.resultPlaceholder: Click Generate or enter a CNPJ to validate
cnpj-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate valid CNPJ numbers for QA testing of Brazilian systems
Validate user-entered CNPJs in form validation logic
Create test data for financial or government applications

items.question: What is a CNPJ?
items.answer: CNPJ (Cadastro Nacional da Pessoa Jurídica) is the Brazilian corporate taxpayer registration number. It consists of 14 digits, including two check digits calculated using a specific algorithm.

items.question: Are the generated CNPJs real?
items.answer: No. The generator creates CNPJ numbers that pass the check digit algorithm but are not necessarily registered with the Brazilian IRS (Receita Federal). They are for testing purposes only.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When testing Brazilian forms that require CNPJ input
When validating CNPJ entries in QA and automation testing
When generating test data for financial or government systems
When developing CNPJ validation logic

regex-tester.title: Regex Tester
regex-tester.description: Test regular expressions against text in real time. Highlights matches visually with full group and capture support.
regex-tester.textPlaceholder: Enter text to test against the regex...
regex-tester.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.
regex-tester.cheatSheetDesc: Click a pattern to use it
regex-tester.howItWorksDesc: Enter a regular expression pattern and test text. Matches are highlighted in yellow below.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Validate email addresses, phone numbers, or URLs with regex
Test pattern matching for data extraction
Debug and refine regular expressions visually

items.question: What is a regular expression?
items.answer: A regular expression (regex) is a sequence of characters that defines a search pattern. It is used for pattern matching within strings, commonly for validation, search, and replace operations.

items.question: What flags are available?
items.answer: Common flags include: g (global - find all matches), i (case-insensitive), m (multiline), and s (dot matches newline).

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When building or debugging regular expressions
When validating input patterns like emails or phone numbers
When testing data extraction patterns
When learning regex syntax

hash-generator.title: Hash Generator
hash-generator.description: Generate cryptographic hashes (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512) instantly as you type. Uses Web Crypto API for SHA hashes.
hash-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate file checksums for integrity verification
Hash passwords for comparison with stored hashes
Create fingerprints for data deduplication

items.question: What is a hash function?
items.answer: A hash function converts input data into a fixed-size string of characters. It is a one-way operation - you cannot reverse a hash to get the original input.

items.question: Which hash algorithm should I use?
items.answer: SHA-256 or SHA-512 are recommended for most modern applications. MD5 and SHA-1 are considered legacy and should only be used for non-security purposes like checksums.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When verifying file integrity with checksums
When comparing hashed values
When generating data fingerprints for deduplication
When testing hash function implementations

color-picker.title: Color Picker & Converter
color-picker.description: Pick a color visually or enter a HEX value. Converts instantly to RGB, RGBA, HSL, HSLA, and CMYK with one-click copy.
color-picker.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, HSL, and CMYK formats
Pick colors for web design and development
Generate RGBA/HSLA values with alpha transparency

items.question: What is alpha transparency?
items.answer: Alpha controls the opacity of a color. 0 is fully transparent, 1 is fully opaque. Used in RGBA and HSLA formats.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When converting colors between different format standards
When picking colors for web development
When generating color values with transparency
When working with design systems and style guides

jwt-generator.title: JWT Generator & Signer
jwt-generator.description: Generate and sign JSON Web Tokens (JWT) with HMAC-SHA256. All processing happens client-side.
jwt-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.
jwt-generator.errorInvalid: Invalid JSON in header or payload

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate JWT tokens for API authentication testing
Create signed tokens with custom claims for development
Test JWT validation logic in your applications

items.question: How does JWT signing work?
items.answer: The tool uses HMAC-SHA256 to create a cryptographic signature of the header and payload using your secret key. The signature ensures the token hasn't been tampered with.

items.question: Is the secret key sent to a server?
items.answer: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser using the Web Crypto API via the jose library. The secret never leaves your device.

items.question: What algorithms are supported?
items.answer: Currently HMAC-SHA256 (HS256) is supported. The header can specify any algorithm, but the signing always uses HS256.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When generating test JWT tokens for API development
When testing JWT validation logic in your applications
When creating tokens with custom claims for development

boundary-value-calculator.title: Boundary Value Calculator
boundary-value-calculator.description: Calculate exact boundary values for test cases from any numeric range. Essential for QA boundary testing.
boundary-value-calculator.errorEmpty: Please enter both Min and Max values.
boundary-value-calculator.errorRange: Min must be less than Max.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Calculate boundary values for an age field with range 18-65
Generate edge cases for a numeric input with min 0 and max 100
Find exact boundary values for a quantity field ranging from 1-50

items.question: What is Boundary Value Analysis?
items.answer: Boundary Value Analysis (BVA) is a testing technique where test cases are designed to include values at the boundaries of input domains. It is based on the observation that errors tend to occur near the extreme values of input ranges.

items.question: Why test at boundaries?
items.answer: Most defects in software occur at the edges of input ranges rather than in the middle. Testing boundary values (Min-1, Min, Min+1, Max-1, Max, Max+1) catches off-by-one errors and range validation bugs.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When writing test cases for numeric input fields with defined ranges
When performing equivalence partitioning and boundary analysis
When validating min/max constraints in forms or APIs

http-status-codes.title: HTTP Status Code Reference
http-status-codes.description: Instant lookup for HTTP status codes. Search by code to see title, description, and when to use.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Look up what a 429 status code means when debugging rate limiting
Quickly reference status codes when writing API error handling
Check the correct status code to return for a new API endpoint

items.question: What are HTTP status codes?
items.answer: HTTP status codes are three-digit numbers returned by a server in response to a client's request. They indicate whether the request was successful, redirected, or resulted in an error.

items.question: What do the categories mean?
items.answer: 1xx are informational, 2xx indicate success, 3xx are redirections, 4xx are client errors (bad request, not found, etc.), and 5xx are server errors (internal error, service unavailable, etc.).

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When debugging API responses and need to understand a status code
When implementing error handling and need the right response codes
When writing API documentation that references status codes

100.title: Continue
100.description: The server has received the request headers and the client should proceed to send the request body.

101.title: Switching Protocols
101.description: The server is switching protocols as requested by the client via the Upgrade header.

102.title: Processing
102.description: The server has received and is processing the request, but no response is available yet.

103.title: Early Hints
103.description: Used to return some response headers before final HTTP message.

200.title: OK
200.description: The request has succeeded. The meaning depends on the method used.

201.title: Created
201.description: The request has been fulfilled and resulted in a new resource being created.

202.title: Accepted
202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but the processing has not been completed.

203.title: Non-Authoritative Information
203.description: The request was successful, but the returned meta-information is not from the original server.

204.title: No Content
204.description: The server successfully processed the request and is not returning any content.

205.title: Reset Content
205.description: The server successfully processed the request, but is not returning any content.

206.title: Partial Content
206.description: The server is delivering only part of the resource due to a Range header in the request.

207.title: Multi-Status
207.description: The message body that follows is an XML message and can contain a number of separate response codes.

208.title: Already Reported
208.description: The members of a DAV binding have already been enumerated in a previous reply.

226.title: IM Used
226.description: The server has fulfilled a GET request for the resource, and the response is a representation of the result of one or more instance-manipulations.

300.title: Multiple Choices
300.description: Indicates multiple options for the resource from which the client may choose.

301.title: Moved Permanently
301.description: This and all future requests should be directed to the given URI.

302.title: Found
302.description: Tells the client to look at another URL.

303.title: See Other
303.description: The response to the request can be found under another URI using a GET method.

304.title: Not Modified
304.description: Indicates the resource has not been modified since the version specified by the request headers.

305.title: Use Proxy
305.description: The requested resource is available only through a proxy.

306.title: Unused
306.description: This status code was used in a previous version of the specification but is now unused.

307.title: Temporary Redirect
307.description: The request should be repeated with another URI but future requests should still use the original URI.

308.title: Permanent Redirect
308.description: The request and all future requests should be repeated using another URI.

400.title: Bad Request
400.description: The server cannot or will not process the request due to an apparent client error.

401.title: Unauthorized
401.description: Authentication is required and has either failed or not been provided.

403.title: Forbidden
403.description: The request was valid, but the server is refusing action.

404.title: Not Found
404.description: The requested resource could not be found but may be available in the future.

405.title: Method Not Allowed
405.description: A request method is not supported for the requested resource.

406.title: Not Acceptable
406.description: The requested resource is capable of generating content not acceptable according to the Accept headers.

407.title: Proxy Authentication Required
407.description: The client must first authenticate itself with the proxy.

408.title: Request Timeout
408.description: The server timed out waiting for the request from the client.

409.title: Conflict
409.description: Indicates that the request could not be processed because of conflict in the current state of the resource.

410.title: Gone
410.description: Indicates that the resource requested is no longer available and will not be available again.

411.title: Length Required
411.description: The request did not specify the length of its content, which is required by the requested resource.

412.title: Precondition Failed
412.description: The server does not meet one of the preconditions that the requester put on the request.

413.title: Content Too Large
413.description: The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.

414.title: URI Too Long
414.description: The URI provided was too long for the server to process.

415.title: Unsupported Media Type
415.description: The request entity has a media type which the server or resource does not support.

416.title: Range Not Satisfiable
416.description: The client has asked for a portion of the file, but the server cannot supply that portion.

417.title: Expectation Failed
417.description: The server cannot meet the requirements of the Expect request-header field.

418.title: I'm a Teapot
418.description: The server refuses the attempt to brew coffee with a teapot.

421.title: Misdirected Request
421.description: The request was directed at a server that is not able to produce a response.

422.title: Unprocessable Content
422.description: The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic errors.

423.title: Locked
423.description: The resource that is being accessed is locked.

424.title: Failed Dependency
424.description: The request failed because it depended on another request and that request failed.

425.title: Too Early
425.description: Indicates that the server is unwilling to risk processing a request that might be replayed.

426.title: Upgrade Required
426.description: The client should switch to a different protocol.

428.title: Precondition Required
428.description: The origin server requires the request to be conditional.

429.title: Too Many Requests
429.description: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.

431.title: Request Header Fields Too Large
431.description: The server is unwilling to process the request because either an individual header field, or all the header fields collectively, are too large.

451.title: Unavailable For Legal Reasons
451.description: A server operator has received a legal demand to deny access to a resource.

500.title: Internal Server Error
500.description: A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered.

501.title: Not Implemented
501.description: The server either does not recognize the request method, or it lacks the ability to fulfil the request.

502.title: Bad Gateway
502.description: The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and received an invalid response from the upstream server.

503.title: Service Unavailable
503.description: The server cannot handle the request (because it is overloaded or down for maintenance).

504.title: Gateway Timeout
504.description: The server was acting as a gateway or proxy and did not receive a timely response from the upstream server.

505.title: HTTP Version Not Supported
505.description: The server does not support the HTTP protocol version used in the request.

506.title: Variant Also Negotiates
506.description: Transparent content negotiation for the request results in a circular reference.

507.title: Insufficient Storage
507.description: The server is unable to store the representation needed to complete the request.

508.title: Loop Detected
508.description: The server detected an infinite loop while processing the request.

510.title: Not Extended
510.description: Further extensions to the request are required for the server to fulfil it.

511.title: Network Authentication Required
511.description: The client needs to authenticate to gain network access.

xpath-css-builder.title: XPath & CSS Selector Builder
xpath-css-builder.description: Build XPath and CSS selectors in real-time by filling in tag, class, ID, and attributes.
xpath-css-builder.realTimeUpdate: Results update in real-time as you type.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Build a locator for a button with class "btn-primary" and id "submit"
Generate an XPath for elements with a custom data-testid attribute
Create a CSS selector for a form input inside a specific container

items.question: What is the difference between XPath and CSS selectors?
items.answer: XPath can traverse up the DOM tree (find parent elements) and supports text-based matching. CSS selectors are generally faster and more concise for simple element selection. Both are widely used in test automation.

items.question: Which should I use for test automation?
items.answer: CSS selectors are preferred for speed and simplicity. Use XPath when you need to navigate parent elements, match by text content, or when CSS selectors cannot uniquely identify an element.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When writing Selenium, Playwright, or Cypress test locators
When building XPath or CSS selectors for web scraping
When debugging element selection issues in test automation

cep-generator.title: CEP Generator & Validator
cep-generator.description: Generate and validate Brazilian CEP postal codes.
cep-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate valid CEP codes for QA testing of Brazilian address forms
Validate user-entered CEPs in form validation logic
Create test data for shipping and logistics applications

items.question: What is a CEP?
items.answer: CEP (Código de Endereçamento Postal) is the Brazilian postal code system. It consists of 8 digits in the format XXXXX-XXX.

items.question: Are the generated CEPs real?
items.answer: The generator creates random 8-digit numbers formatted as CEPs. They may or may not correspond to real addresses in Brazil.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When testing Brazilian forms that require CEP input
When validating CEP entries in QA and automation testing
When generating test data for shipping or logistics systems

renavam-generator.title: Renavam Generator & Validator
renavam-generator.description: Generate and validate Brazilian Renavam vehicle registration numbers.
renavam-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate valid Renavam numbers for QA testing of vehicle registration systems
Validate user-entered Renavam numbers in form validation logic
Create test data for automotive or government applications

items.question: What is a Renavam?
items.answer: Renavam (Registro Nacional de Veículos Automotores) is the Brazilian national vehicle registration number. It consists of 11 digits including a check digit.

items.question: Are the generated Renavam numbers real?
items.answer: The generator creates valid Renavam numbers using the official check digit algorithm, but they are not necessarily registered with DENATRAN.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When testing Brazilian vehicle registration forms
When validating Renavam entries in QA and automation testing
When generating test data for automotive systems

pix-key-generator.title: PIX Key Generator
pix-key-generator.description: Generate fake PIX keys for testing Brazilian payment systems.
pix-key-generator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate test PIX keys for payment system development
Create fake PIX data for QA testing of Brazilian banking apps
Test PIX key validation logic in payment forms

items.question: What is a PIX key?
items.answer: PIX is Brazil's instant payment system. PIX keys can be random (EVP), CPF, email, phone, or key. This tool generates random EVP keys, formatted CPFs, and fake emails for testing.

items.question: Are these real PIX keys?
items.answer: No. These are fake keys generated for testing purposes only. They are not registered in the Brazilian Central Bank's PIX system.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When developing or testing PIX payment integrations
When creating test data for Brazilian fintech applications
When validating PIX key formats in QA testing

items.question: Does it handle negative numbers?

byte-converter.description: Convert between Bytes, KB, MB, GB, and TB storage units.
byte-converter.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Convert file sizes between storage units
Calculate memory requirements for applications
Verify network transfer speeds in different units

items.question: What conversion units are supported?
items.answer: The converter supports Bytes (B), Kilobytes (KB), Megabytes (MB), Gigabytes (GB), and Terabytes (TB).

items.question: What is the conversion factor?
items.answer: Each unit is 1024 times the previous unit. For example, 1 KB = 1024 Bytes, 1 MB = 1024 KB, etc.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When converting file sizes between different units
When calculating storage requirements
When comparing network bandwidth or memory allocations

items.question: What cURL flags are supported?

items.question: Does it handle complex cURL commands?

user-agent-parser.description: Decode User-Agent strings. Extract OS, browser, version, and device type from any UA string.
user-agent-parser.howToUseContent: A User-Agent (UA) string is a text string that web browsers send to websites to identify the browser, operating system, and device. Paste any UA string into the field above to decode it. You can find User-Agent strings in your browser's developer tools or HTTP request logs.
user-agent-parser.realExampleString: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
user-agent-parser.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Parse User-Agent strings from HTTP request logs
Identify browser and device types from analytics data
Debug device-specific issues by analyzing UA strings

items.question: What information is extracted?
items.answer: The parser extracts the Operating System, Browser name, Browser version, and Device type (Desktop, Mobile, Tablet, or Unknown).

items.question: Which browsers are supported?
items.answer: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, and Opera are detected. The parser also handles most common User-Agent string formats from mobile devices.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When analyzing User-Agent strings from server logs
When debugging browser-specific issues in web applications
When testing responsive designs for different device types

items.question: What is a MIME type?
items.answer: A MIME (Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions) type indicates the nature and format of a document or file. It's used in HTTP headers to tell browsers how to handle responses.

items.question: How many MIME types are included?
items.answer: The tool includes over 100 common MIME types covering web, images, audio, video, documents, archives, fonts, data formats, and programming languages.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?

When configuring file upload validation
When setting up web server MIME type configurations

chmod-calculator.title: Chmod Calculator
chmod-calculator.description: Calculate Linux file permissions with a visual checkbox interface. Generates octal and symbolic notation.
chmod-calculator.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.
chmod-calculator.explanationValues: Each permission has a numeric value:
chmod-calculator.explanationGroups: The total for each group is the sum of the active permissions:
chmod-calculator.explanationExample: Example: Owner has r+w+x (4+2+1=7), Group has r+x (4+0+1=5), Public has r+x (4+0+1=5) =

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Calculate permissions for a web server directory (755)
Set secure file permissions for SSH keys (600)
Generate chmod commands for deployment scripts

items.question: What do the octal numbers mean?
items.answer: Each digit represents permissions for Owner, Group, and Public respectively. 4=read, 2=write, 1=execute. For example, 755 means Owner has rwx (7), Group has r-x (5), and Public has r-x (5).

items.question: What is the difference between octal and symbolic notation?
items.answer: Octal uses numbers (e.g., 755), while symbolic uses letters (e.g., rwxr-xr-x). Both represent the same permissions but in different formats.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When setting file permissions on Linux/Unix systems
When debugging permission-related issues on servers
When writing deployment or CI/CD scripts that need chmod commands

items.question: What are common aspect ratios?
items.answer: 16:9 (widescreen), 4:3 (standard), 1:1 (square), 21:9 (ultrawide), and 3:2 (photography) are the most common ratios used in media and design.

bdd-gherkin-builder.title: BDD / Gherkin Snippet Builder
bdd-gherkin-builder.description: Build Gherkin scenarios visually with Given, When, Then steps. Perfectly formatted output.
bdd-gherkin-builder.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Build Gherkin scenarios for login functionality testing
Create BDD specifications for API endpoint validation
Generate Given/When/Then steps for shopping cart workflows

items.question: What is Gherkin syntax?
items.answer: Gherkin is a plain-text language used to describe business behavior in BDD (Behavior-Driven Development). It uses Given/When/Then keywords to structure test scenarios.

items.question: What are Given, When, Then?
items.answer: Given sets up the initial context, When describes the action or event, and Then specifies the expected outcome. 'And' can be used to extend any of these steps.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When writing BDD test scenarios for Cucumber or SpecFlow
When creating living documentation for application behavior
When collaborating with non-technical stakeholders on requirements

items.question: Is this a full HTML parser?
items.answer: This is a lightweight client-side converter. It handles common HTML elements but may not support complex nested structures or custom attributes.

text-binary-converter.title: Text ⇄ Binary Converter
text-binary-converter.description: Convert text to binary (UTF-8) and binary back to text. Useful for encoding, debugging, and learning.
text-binary-converter.textToBinaryPlaceholder: Enter text to convert to binary...
text-binary-converter.errorInvalid: Invalid input. Please check your input and try again.
text-binary-converter.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

items.question: How does text to binary conversion work?
items.answer: Each character is converted to its 8-bit binary representation using UTF-8 encoding. For example, the letter 'A' has the ASCII value 65, which is 01000001 in binary.

items.question: Does this support Unicode characters?
items.answer: Yes, this tool uses UTF-8 encoding, so it supports all Unicode characters including emojis, accented characters, and non-Latin scripts.

items.question: What format should binary input be in?
items.answer: Binary values should be 8 bits each (e.g., 01000001), separated by spaces. Each 8-bit group represents one byte/character.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When learning about binary representation of text
When debugging data encoding or transmission issues
When you need to convert text to binary for educational purposes

markdown-pdf-converter.title: Markdown → PDF
markdown-pdf-converter.description: Convert Markdown documents to beautifully styled PDF files. Perfect for reports, documentation, and professional documents.
markdown-pdf-converter.errorCopy: Failed to copy. Please try again.

examples.title: Examples of use
items: Generate professional reports from Markdown specifications
Export documentation to print-ready PDF format
Create polished presentations from Markdown content

items.question: How does the PDF export work?
items.answer: The tool converts your Markdown to styled HTML and opens it in a new window. The browser's print dialog then lets you save as PDF. This uses the built-in window.print() API — no server-side processing required.

items.question: What Markdown features are supported?
items.answer: Headers (h1-h6), bold, italic, links, images, code blocks, inline code, lists (ordered and unordered), blockquotes, horizontal rules, and tables.

items.question: Why use this instead of the Markdown ⇄ HTML converter?
items.answer: This tool is specifically designed for PDF output with professional typography and styling. It includes Google Fonts (Inter, JetBrains Mono), carefully crafted CSS for print media, and a streamlined workflow focused on document export.

whenToUse.title: When to use this tool?
items: When you need to export Markdown to a professional-looking PDF
When creating reports or documentation for print
When you want beautifully styled code blocks and typography in your documents
